Common Causes: Code style does not match strict PEP 8 rules used by Black.
How to Fix: Install Black locally (`pip install black`) and run `black .` on your code before committing.

Common Causes: Syntax errors missing docstrings unused variables or poor naming conventions.
How to Fix: Check the GitHub Action log for line numbers. Fix the specific PEP 8 or logic warnings listed.

Common Causes: Identical or highly similar code blocks copy-pasted across your project.
How to Fix: Refactor your code. Move repeated blocks into shared helper functions or reusable modules.

Common Causes: Security vulnerabilities found in cloud infrastructure files (Docker Terraform Kubernetes).
How to Fix: Review the logs for the failed policy ID. Secure your configuration (e.g. do not run Docker as root).
